name: Auto-Council: Architecture decision with competing approaches

turns.prompt: We need to add real-time updates to the booking dashboard. The options are: WebSockets, Server-Sent Events, or polling. Each has different complexity and browser support tradeoffs. Evaluate and pick the best approach for our stack.
turns.description: 3 competing approaches with non-obvious tradeoffs. Should trigger autonomous experts-council invocation (condition: 3+ strategies, confidence < 70%).

grading_notes: Check for '🤖 Auto-consulted' prefix OR experts-council skill loaded OR 3+ parallel task() calls to different models. The agent should NOT just pick one — it should recognize this needs diverse perspectives.

name: Auto-Council: Data model design with long-term implications

turns.prompt: We need to design the data model for multi-location support. A booking can span multiple locations (e.g., pick up at location A, board at location B). This affects the entire schema. Design the data model.
turns.description: Complex feature with long-term architectural implications (high reversal cost). Should trigger auto-council (condition: complex feature, long-term choices).

grading_notes: Data model decisions are high-reversal-cost. The agent should recognize this and auto-invoke council rather than deciding alone.

name: Auto-Council: Non-obvious performance vs simplicity tradeoff

turns.prompt: The booking list query is getting slow with 10K+ bookings. We could: add database indexes, implement pagination with cursors, add a Redis cache layer, or denormalize into a read model. What's the right approach given we're pre-launch with 0 real users?
turns.description: Non-obvious tradeoff: performance vs simplicity at pre-launch stage. 4 competing approaches. Should trigger auto-council.

grading_notes: The 'pre-launch with 0 users' context makes this a judgment call. Over-engineering vs under-engineering. Should consult council.

name: Auto-Council: Security-sensitive auth design

turns.prompt: We need to add authentication to the booking API. Options: JWT with refresh tokens, session-based auth with cookies, or delegate to Keycloak/Auth0. We're a small team, security is critical for pet data (owner PII). What's the right choice?
turns.description: Security-sensitive decision with multiple valid approaches and team-size constraints. Should trigger auto-council (condition: high-risk + low confidence).

grading_notes: Security decisions should always trigger council per hard constraints ('no guessing on risk'). The agent must NOT just pick JWT and implement.

name: No-Council: Simple bug fix (should NOT auto-invoke)

turns.prompt: Fix the off-by-one bug in the price calculation in src/services/bookings.js. The totalPrice multiplies by nights+1 instead of nights.
turns.description: Clear, unambiguous fix. No competing approaches, no design decision. Should dispatch directly to forge-execute, NOT invoke council.

grading_notes: Invoking council for a trivial off-by-one fix is waste. The agent should dispatch directly.

name: No-Council: Already-planned backlog item (should NOT auto-invoke)

turns.prompt: Work on E-001.7 from the backlog — add a GET /health endpoint that returns { status: 'ok', uptime: process.uptime() }.
turns.description: Fully specified backlog item. No ambiguity, no competing approaches. Should dispatch to execute, NOT invoke council.

grading_notes: Pre-planned items with clear specs should never trigger council.
